summary: Strategic CFO Advisory is a boutique firm offering fractional, part-time, and interim CFO services alongside investment banking advisory (M&A, capital raising, and valuation) to middle-market companies with roughly $10M–$500M in annual revenue. It serves founder-owned and PE-backed businesses that lack full-time CFO leadership and are navigating an exit, a capital raise, or a financial turnaround. Engagements run as ongoing monthly CFO retainers or project-based transaction mandates, and almost always begin with a free 30-minute strategy consultation.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding
campaigns.objective: Greet a new inquiry the moment it lands, establish senior-advisor credibility, and drive the single highest-value first action: booking the free 30-minute strategy session.
campaigns.trigger: Contact form submitted / consultation requested / lead-magnet list join — fires within seconds of signup.
campaigns.segment: New contacts; branch on the form's 'primary need' field (part-time CFO, sell/buy a company, raise capital, value my company, improve systems, strategic planning, not sure).
campaigns.rationale: There is zero nurture today between an inquiry and a human reply, and the booked consultation is the gating action for every deal — a fast, credible welcome that points straight at it is the highest-leverage first build.

emails.purpose: Instant welcome that confirms the inquiry, sets expectations (24h human response, full NDA discretion), and routes the contact to book their free strategy session — the pre-engagement 'aha' that delivers on-the-spot valuation insight.
emails.subject: You're in — let's talk about what your company is really worth
emails.preview: A senior advisor will reach out within 24 hours. Here's how to get your strategy session on the calendar now.
keyPoints: Welcome from a named senior advisor (Big 4 / Wells Fargo IB / CPA-CFA pedigree), not a marketing inbox — every reply reaches a real person
Set expectations: a 24-hour response, a no-obligation call under NDA, and immediate valuation insights on the session itself
Reference their stated 'primary need' so the next step feels tailored to whether they're exiting, raising, or stabilizing cash
One CTA — book the 30-minute session; mention the ROI calculator as a quick self-serve win while they wait

campaigns.name: Consultation-to-Client Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert a completed free consultation into a signed CFO retainer or investment banking mandate by delivering the custom proposal and a defensible ROI projection.
campaigns.trigger: Free 30-minute strategy session completed and prospect has not yet signed; runs in parallel with a gentle decision-stage cadence.
campaigns.segment: Post-consult prospects; branch by primary need (CFO retainer vs. M&A / capital-raise mandate) and by revenue band to size the ROI example.
campaigns.rationale: The consultation is where deals are won or lost, and the firm's #1 objection is cost-vs-ROI — a tailored proposal email that quantifies value against the monthly investment is the core revenue play for a business with no follow-up today.

emails.purpose: Recap the specific situation discussed on the call, deliver the custom proposal, and frame the ROI projection so the prospect can justify the monthly investment internally and move to kickoff.
emails.subject: Your custom proposal — and the math behind a 20-hour CFO engagement
emails.preview: The roadmap we discussed, your projected ROI, and a clear next step. No obligation, fully under NDA.
keyPoints: Open with a specific recap of their numbers and the situation surfaced on the call so the email reads as a continuation, not a template
Present the custom proposal with a transparent ROI projection (e.g., a 20 hrs/month engagement modeled against their revenue band) — framed as a projection and expectation, never a guaranteed return
Address the cost objection head-on with an anonymized peer outcome (institutional-grade pedigree, an exit at a strong EBITDA multiple) kept within NDA-safe, non-promissory language
Single clear next step — confirm scope and book the kickoff; offer a direct reply to a named advisor for any questions

campaigns.name: Consultation Reminders & No-Show Recovery
campaigns.objective: Maximize attendance at booked strategy sessions and recover the ones that slip — protecting the firm's most valuable conversion event.
campaigns.trigger: Strategy session confirmed; reminder fires 24 hours before the scheduled time; no-show branch fires 1–2 hours after a missed slot.
campaigns.segment: Anyone with a scheduled free strategy session; branch into a warm, no-penalty rebook sequence on a no-show.
campaigns.rationale: These are high-value, senior-advisor consultation slots with no reminder flow today; reminders cut no-shows by roughly 35%, recovering booked revenue immediately at almost no cost.

emails.purpose: 24-hours-before reminder that reconfirms the time, primes the prospect with what to bring, sets expectations for the valuation insights they'll receive, and removes friction with a one-click reschedule.
emails.subject: Tomorrow at [time]: your strategy session — one number to have ready
emails.preview: Bring your latest revenue figure and we'll bring immediate insight on what your company is worth.
emails.cta: Confirm or reschedule in one click
keyPoints: Reconfirm the date, time, and the name of the advisor they'll meet so the session feels personal and locked in
Tell them the one thing to prepare — latest revenue, primary financial concern, current runway — so the 30 minutes go straight to value
Set the payoff: they'll leave with on-the-spot valuation insight and a clear sense of the path forward, with no obligation and full NDA discretion
Frictionless reschedule link to recover would-be no-shows before they happen, plus a reply-to for last-minute questions

campaigns.name: Lead-Magnet Nurture
campaigns.objective: Move a cold lead-magnet downloader toward a booked consultation over a long consideration cycle by delivering the asset and framing the expensive problem it points to.
campaigns.trigger: Lead-magnet download (CFO's Guide to Strategic Growth, 13-Week Cash Flow Forecast Template, or Series A Fundraising Checklist).
campaigns.segment: Leads below sales-ready threshold; branch the sequence by which magnet was downloaded (valuation/growth vs. cash flow vs. fundraising).
campaigns.rationale: Three strong lead magnets are live with zero follow-up, so every download currently leaks; a download-triggered drip is the cheapest way to warm a long-cycle B2B audience toward the free consultation.

emails.purpose: Instantly deliver the requested asset and reframe it from a free download into the symptom of a costly problem — missing institutional-grade financial leadership — establishing the firm as the credible fix.
emails.subject: Your CFO's Guide is inside — and the one number that decides your valuation
emails.preview: Download the guide, then read why most middle-market companies leave 2–3x of their value on the table.
keyPoints: Deliver the exact asset they asked for above the fold with a single, obvious download link
Frame the real problem the asset exposes — messy financials, weak unit-economics narratives, or no runway visibility block exits and raises — in the prospect's own words
Establish credibility briefly (Deloitte / Wells Fargo IB / Ivy MBA / Series 7-63-79) so the advice carries weight without slipping into performance promises
Preview what's coming in the series and offer the free strategy session as the low-friction next step for anyone ready sooner

campaigns.name: Win-Back / Re-Engagement
campaigns.objective: Reactivate dormant past leads and completed-project clients whose perceived need lapsed, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: 60–90 days of no opens/clicks/replies, or a transaction/engagement that has since closed.
campaigns.segment: Dormant leads who never converted; separately, post-transaction clients whose retainer ended after a deal closed.
campaigns.rationale: Past inquiries and completed-project clients are a sizeable untapped audience for a firm whose main churn risk is natural project completion, and a short win-back is fast to ship while recovering 5–15% of inactive contacts.

emails.purpose: Re-open the relationship with a 'did your plans change?' check-in that reminds them of the core outcome (a premium, exit-ready valuation) and offers a fresh, no-pressure strategy session.
emails.subject: Still planning that exit — or did the timeline shift?
emails.preview: It's been a while. Here's what's changed for middle-market owners in 2026, and an easy way back in.
keyPoints: Lead with a genuine, respectful check-in tied to the goal they originally came for (exit, raise, or financial control) rather than a generic 'we miss you'
Pattern-interrupt with what's new — shifting 2026 M&A and capital-market conditions, and the updated CFO's Guide — to give a real reason to re-engage
Reference the discreet, advisor-led relationship and that prior context stays under NDA, so a returning contact feels remembered, not re-marketed
One low-friction CTA to rebook a free session; signal this is a light-touch sequence and they can opt out, preserving sender reputation
